senses.gloss: to unfasten; to untie; to unknot; to unravel; to disentangle; to loosen
to unfasten; to untie; to unknot; to unravel; to disentangle; to loosen; to solve, to find an answer to or a solution for (something, such as a problem); to untangle; to crack
to liquefy; to dissolve
to liquefy; to dissolve; to turn into a solution
to invalidate, to deprive of effective legal or otherwise binding power; to dissolve
to invalidate, to deprive of effective legal or otherwise binding power; to dissolve; to breach (a contract, an agreement, an oath, and the like); to break
to settle; to approach

examples.text: فَلَأَنْتَ أَنْتَ إِذَاْ حَلَلْتَ بــ: طَيْبَةٍ
examples.english: For when you, approach goodness(طيبة) (one of the names of Madinah)

senses.gloss: to dismount; to stop, to halt
to stay, to settle
to visit
to live in, to reside in (a place)

examples.text: وَلَا يَزَالُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا تُصِيبُهُم بِمَا صَنَعُوا قَارِعَةٌ أَوْ تَحُلُّ قَرِيبًا مِّن دَارِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِيَ وَعْدُ اللَّهِ
examples.english: If there were a recitation that could cause mountains to move, or the earth to split, or the dead to speak, «it would have been this Quran». But all matters are by Allah’s Will. Have the believers not yet realized that had Allah willed, He could have guided all of humanity? And disasters will continue to afflict the disbelievers or strike close to their homes for their misdeeds, until Allah’s promise comes to pass. Surely Allah never fails in His promise.

senses.gloss: to occupy, to take (someone's place)

examples.text: كان في أحد الأكياس تشكيلة من البسكويت القديم الذي اندثر من بقالات صنعاء وغيرها من المدن. هذه الأنواع من البسكويت كانت متوفرة عندما كنت صغيراً وعمري على ما أذكر 4–7 سنوات، ثمّ اختفت بالتدريج، وحلّت محلّها أصناف أخرى.
examples.english: In one of the bags there was a pack of ancient biscuits the kind of which disappeared from most grocers of Ṣanʻāʼ and other cities. This type of biscuit was widespread when I small and my age, as I remember, 4–7, then it left sight gradually, and other kinds took its place.

senses.gloss: to descend, to go down
to occur, to happen
to befall [with بِ (bi) or عَلَى (ʕalā) ‘someone’] (of punishment, suffering, hatred or something else negative)

examples.text: كُلُوا مِنْ طَيِّبَاتِ مَا رَزَقْنَاكُمْ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا فِيهِ فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبِي وَمَنْ يَحْلِلْ عَلَيْهِ غَضَبِي فَقَدْ هَوَىٰ
examples.english: Eat of the good things wherewith We have provided you, and transgress not in respect thereof lest My wrath come upon you: and he on whom My wrath cometh, he is lost indeed.

senses.gloss: to overwhelm [with عَلَى (ʕalā) ‘someone’] (of sleep)
to arrive, to begin (of a season or time)
to become incarnate [with فِي (fī) ‘in someone’] (of God)
to be or become permissible or lawful

examples.text: وَلَا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَأْخُذُوا مِمَّا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ شَيْئًا
examples.english: And it is not permissible for you that ye take aught of what ye have given them.

senses.gloss: verbal noun of حَلَّ (ḥalla) (form I)
a solution

examples.english: For every problem, there are a thousand solutions.

senses.gloss: sesame, or sesame oil

examples.text: وتعجن بدهن اللوز أو الحل، الشربة منه حبة إلى دانقين بسمن بقر أو بدهن الحل.
examples.english: And knead with almond oil or sesame oil, the dose of it is one pill to two dāniq, with butter or with sesame oil.

examples.text: دهن الحلّ هو عند الرازي غير دهن السمسم وهو عند الإسرائيلي وفي كتاب مسيح دهن الحلّ أي الشيرج الذي لم يقشر سمسمه.
examples.english: Duhn al-ḥall is according to Rhazes not sesame oil but according to al-Isrāʾīlīy and Masīh duhn al-ḥall or šīraj is that where the sesame has not been peeled.

etymology.text:
From the root ح ل ل (ḥ l l). Compare Hebrew חל.
The meaning of “sesame oil” may derive via a chain of calques from Akkadian 𒉌𒄑 (ellum), which is also homographic to 𒉌𒄑 (šamnum, “oil”), that, to build a bridge, might suggest that Aramaic חָלָא / ܚܠܐ (ḥallā) also had this meaning but has consistently been misread as more common “vinegar”, the etymon of Arabic خَلّ (ḵall, “vinegar”), if not other homographs, due to complete merger of ḥ and ḫ more likely than in Arabic itself where it also happens due to taṣḥīf.

body.text: is a pl. [or coll. gen. n.], and its sing. [or n. un.] is ↓
(Mgh,) of a sword, (S, Mgh, Msb, K,) as also ↓
, (K,) or of a lamp, and of other things: (Mgh:) accord. to Lth,
with which one decks a woman
in relation to other things, to a sword [for instance], and the like: (TA:) the pl. of
; (S, K;) or, accord. to IF, it has no pl. (Msb.)
A cry by which a she-camel is chidden;
; like as a he-camel is by the cry
by which female camels are chidden;
, and, when in connexion with a following word,
.) One says, in chiding the she-camel, ↓
On! mayest thou not gain any great benefit:
like as one says, in chiding the he-camel,
it is one of the best kinds of pasture of the people of the desert for camels and horses; and when its produce appears, it resembles eared corn:
seed-produce, in its manner of growth;
but this, says Az, is a mistake: (TA:) n. un. with
that is between the two bulls:
of the dial. of El Yemen. (TA.)
